response.justification: Candidate lacks the two hardest non-negotiables for this role: native-level Japanese proficiency (not listed at all) and recent professional C programming for embedded/Linux-kernel work. Background is overwhelmingly web/full-stack (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang) with only a brief C/C++ stint at National Instruments in 2014-2015 on a desktop IDE, not embedded networking. No demonstrated experience with MACsec, 802.1X, L2/L3 switching, NETCONF, SNMP, chip-vendor SDKs, or Linux kernel modification.
strengths: 10+ years of professional software development experience
Some historical C/C++ exposure at National Instruments
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, testing culture
concerns: No evidence of Japanese language proficiency (hard requirement)
No embedded firmware, switch, or networking-protocol experience
No Linux kernel source modification or security-protocol (MACsec/802.1X) work
